- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Compose-Contract-Injection — Consolidated Plan (OPUS)
2	
3	Commit ground truth: `/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent` @ `b67506a`
4	(`b67506a399b6102223cf9aad0be4b5e9b9828544`). Every code claim below was
5	cross-checked against the real tree; line numbers are from that commit.
6	
7	## Which models returned
8	
9	- **codex.md** — returned (full). ("The fix is mostly wiring, not a generator
10	  rewrite…"). Its content is byte-identical to the tail of `codex.err`.
11	- **kimi.md** — returned (full). Kimi K3, self-labeled, with usage block.
12	- **fable.md** — **DID NOT RETURN.** No `fable.md` exists in the pack directory
13	  (`ls` confirms absence). Nothing in this plan is attributed to Fable.
14	- **pack.md** — the grounded context pack (not a model); used as the code map and
15	  re-verified against source.
16	
17	Both returning models (codex, kimi) independently converge on the same shape:
18	the plumbing already exists and is starved at one call site; the fix is ~80%
19	threading + a drop-point repair, ~20% projection glue. I agree, **with one
20	correction neither model made** (see Adjudication §1).
21	
22	---
23	
24	## KEY CORRECTION vs. both models — the compose projection object already exists
25	
26	Both codex and kimi propose **building a new object** — codex a
27	`ComposeProjection(...)` bundle; kimi a new `ResearchContract.to_compose_projection()`
28	returning a new frozen `ComposeProjection`. **This is redundant and would be
29	over-engineering.** The real tree already ships the compile method and object:
30	
31	`src/polaris_graph/planning/compose_render_projection.py`
32	- `class ComposeRenderProjection` (line 118) — plain-data compose+render view.
33	- `from_contract(contract)` (line 201) — pure, fail-open compiler. It **already**
34	  populates:
35	  - `tone/audience/point_of_view/hedging` from `_TONE_DIMS/_AUDIENCE_DIMS/_POV_DIMS/_HEDGING_DIMS`
36	  - `doc_type` from `_DOCTYPE_DIMS` (line 94: includes `deliverable.kind`,
37	    `deliverable.document_type`, `deliverable.structure`, `document_type`, `kind`)
38	  - `required_titles` + `ordered` from `contract.sections` — **only** sections with
39	    `exact_title_lock=True` (lines 225-256; a required *topic* is never a heading)
40	  - `length_note` (planning context, never a cap)
41	- `voice_advisory()` (line 142) — the prose-only voice advisory string.
42	- `document_type()` (line 169), `required_titles`, `render_plan()` (line 179).
43	- `compose_voice_advisory(proj)` (line 294) — the generator-seam entrypoint; the
44	  generator already calls it (multi_section_generator.py line 9836).
45	
46	So the minimal plan **reuses `from_contract`**; it does NOT add a new projection
47	class or a new `to_compose_projection()` method. What is genuinely missing:
48	
49	1. The **gate drop-point** still drops `deliverable.kind` and voice dims and never
50	   populates `contract.sections` → so `from_contract` reads an empty projection.
51	2. The **call site** (scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py line 15861) threads none of
52	   `compose_projection` / `deliverable_spec` / `scope_spec`.
53	3. The **section advisory** carries voice only — no doc-type framing line, no
54	   per-section role. `voice_advisory()` must gain an additive doc-type preamble;
55	   per-section role is a separate additive append.
56	4. `ComposeRenderProjection` has no `deliverable_spec` / `scope_spec` view the
57	   outline seam wants (`_call_outline` reads `_spec_read(deliverable_spec,
58	   "required_sections", [])` and `build_requirements_block(deliverable_spec,
59	   scope_spec)`). We expose thin adapters, not a new object.
60	
61	---
62	
63	## (a) Contract-carry change — unblock kind, populate sections, carry voice
64	
65	**File:** `src/polaris_graph/planning/research_planning_gate.py` (degraded /
66	`compiler_degraded` constructor, ~lines 460–519). The admit tuple today (line 491):
67	
68	```python
69	if cand.dimension not in (
70	    "deliverable.format", "deliverable.length", "rhetoric.tone",
71	):
72	    continue
73	```
74	
75	Two defects: (i) `deliverable.kind` and voice dims are absent → dropped; (ii) it
76	matches on **raw** `cand.dimension` while appending `cand.canonical_dimension()`
77	(line ~486) — so a `document_type`/`kind` variant is dropped even after "adding
78	kind" unless we match on the canonical form (kimi's point #1, verified against the
79	constructor: it does append `cand.canonical_dimension()`).
80	
81	**Change — one source of truth, matched on the canonical dimension:**
82	
83	```python
84	# Single admit set, reconciled with the dims the compose projection reads
85	# (compose_render_projection._TONE_DIMS/_AUDIENCE_DIMS/_POV_DIMS/_HEDGING_DIMS/
86	#  _DOCTYPE_DIMS) + format/length. Importing those frozensets keeps admit and
87	# projection from silently diverging (a half-fix no task-72 test catches).
88	from src.polaris_graph.planning.compose_render_projection import (
89	    _TONE_DIMS, _AUDIENCE_DIMS, _POV_DIMS, _HEDGING_DIMS, _DOCTYPE_DIMS,
90	)
91	_DELIVERABLE_ADMIT_DIMS = frozenset(
92	    {"deliverable.format", "deliverable.length"}
93	    | _DOCTYPE_DIMS | _TONE_DIMS | _AUDIENCE_DIMS | _POV_DIMS | _HEDGING_DIMS
94	)
95	...
96	if cand.canonical_dimension() not in _DELIVERABLE_ADMIT_DIMS:
97	    continue
98	```
99	
100	(If importing the underscored frozensets is judged too intimate, promote them to a
101	public name in `compose_render_projection.py` and import that. Do **not** copy the
102	sets — two hand-maintained tables will drift.)
103	
104	**Kind force:** admitted `deliverable.kind` stays `force=FORCE_PREFER` exactly like
105	the other deliverable candidates (the constructor already sets FORCE_PREFER, line
106	~509). This is correct and lossless: an inferred kind *cannot* be hard anyway
107	(`HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS` invariant). Do NOT add `deliverable.kind` to
108	`_AUTHORITATIVE_DIMENSIONS` (line ~296) — that frozenset feeds the monotonic
109	retrieval merge; a deliverable preference there is a category leak across
110	`STAGE_OWNERS`. (kimi #3; confirmed `_AUTHORITATIVE_DIMENSIONS` is source/date/
111	exclusion only.)
112	
113	**Sections:** admit **explicit, user-named** section-title candidates into
114	`SectionRequirement` objects. Preserve verbatim: title term (with real
115	origin/span), encounter order (unless an explicit order was stated), purpose,
116	`required`, coverage links. `exact_title_lock` **only** when the title term's
117	origin ∈ `HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS` — reuse `SectionRequirement.from_dict`'s existing
118	downgrade rule; the constructor must not bypass it (codex; kimi). **Do not
119	manufacture `contract.sections` from the kind** — "decision memo" does not prove
120	the user typed headings "Options"/"Recommendation"; kind→section skeletons are
121	*preferences*, routed through the soft outline channel (§c), never `sections`
122	with locks (codex explicit; kimi allows an *inferred, unlocked, disclosed* default
123	skeleton only when kind itself is explicit — I adjudicate to **codex's stricter
124	line**: keep `sections` for explicit user titles only; kind-derived roles live in
125	the projection's preference channel. Simpler, and it removes an invention surface).
126	
127	**Voice:** already handled by the admit-set expansion above — voice terms are
128	deliverable terms on the tone/audience/pov/hedging dims; once admitted,
129	`from_contract` reads them. No separate work.
130	
131	**SHA honesty (both models, correct — surface it, do not smuggle it):** admitting
132	kind/voice/sections changes the *degraded* contract's `to_dict()` → its
133	`contract_sha256` changes vs. today. That is the fix landing, not a regression.
134	State it in the change note. Assert the invariant that `to_scope_terms()` output
135	is **unchanged** by deliverable admission (deliverable terms don't enter scope), so
136	retrieval behavior does not drift even when caches key on the SHA. Legacy
137	non-degraded contracts are unaffected (generic-IR fields still serialize only when
138	non-default → their SHAs are stable).
139	
140	## (b) Compose projection + injection point into the section-writer prompt
141	
142	**Object / builder:** REUSE `ComposeRenderProjection.from_contract(_shape_contract)`.
143	No new class.
144	
145	**Build once, thread at the call site.** `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py`: the
146	contract is already in scope as `_shape_contract` (line 14396, assigned from the
147	gate artifact at 14412). Immediately before the `generate_multi_section_report`
148	call (line 15861), compile the projection and thread three kwargs:
149	
150	```python
151	from src.polaris_graph.planning.compose_render_projection import from_contract
152	_compose_proj = from_contract(_shape_contract)   # empty/inert if contract is None/shapeless
153	...
154	multi = await generate_multi_section_report(
155	    research_question=q["question"],
156	    evidence=evidence_for_gen,
157	    ...
158	    compose_projection=_compose_proj,
159	    deliverable_spec=_deliverable_spec_from(_compose_proj),
160	    scope_spec=_scope_spec_from(_shape_contract),
161	    ...
162	)
163	```
164	
165	`_deliverable_spec_from` / `_scope_spec_from` are **thin adapters** (see §c) — a
166	dict `{"required_sections": proj.required_titles, ...}` and a scope-emphasis view.
167	They are the only new glue and are trivially testable.
168	
169	**Injection point — file:function and exact seam:**
170	`src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py`, function `_call_section`,
171	line **4071–4072**:
172	
173	```python
174	if voice_advisory_text:
175	    system = f"{system}\n\n{voice_advisory_text}"
176	```
177	
178	This is the additive prompt-preamble seam. It appends AFTER the frozen
179	`SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE` (selected at 4055) and after the domain advisory
180	(4066). Voice/compose guidance is therefore strictly a suffix on the system prompt;
181	the faithfulness contract (rules 1–8: evidence-only, every-sentence-cited, verbatim
182	numbers, "do not write a heading") is the unchanged prefix.
183	
184	**Two additive layers into the advisory the generator computes at line 9830-9838:**
185	
186	1. **Doc-type framing (global, per-report).** Extend `voice_advisory()` (or a new
187	   sibling `compose_advisory()` called at the same seam) so the string returned to
188	   `_voice_advisory_text` ALSO carries a doc-type framing line when
189	   `proj.doc_type` resolves. The framing prose is **DATA, not control flow** —
190	   store a per-archetype `compose_directive` string next to
191	   `report_skeleton.ARCHETYPES`, looked up by the resolved archetype key (reuse
192	   `report_skeleton.KIND_SYNONYMS` to resolve `doc_type` → key; unmapped ⇒ opaque
193	   ⇒ generic/default directive + disclosure). No `if kind == "memo"` in code.
194	
195	2. **Per-section role (per-section).** The once-built `_voice_advisory_text` cannot
196	   carry "this is section 2 of N, 'Options Compared', its job: …". Append the role
197	   line at the `_run_section` → `_call_section` boundary from
198	   `proj` keyed by the current `section.title`. Still one additive append; still
199	   `""` ⇒ inert. (kimi #6; codex "compose_section_advisory(projection, section)".)
200	
201	**Hard rules for the preamble (both models, and I enforce them as tests in §f):**
202	- **Directive-only, zero content.** No claims, no numbers, no `ev_\d+`, no canned
203	  sentences ("In this memo we recommend…"). A canned content sentence has no span
204	  ⇒ `strict_verify` drops it ⇒ retry churn. Invariant test: rendered advisory
205	  matches neither `\d` nor `ev_`.
206	- **Length precedence.** `SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE` rule 8 targets 10–18
207	  sentences; `length_note` is already worded as "planning context (not a hard
208	  cap)" (line 285) — keep it advisory; if a compose length directive is added it
209	  must explicitly supersede rule 8 or say nothing, never silently contradict it.
210	- **Coverage > Insight > Readability.** Voice may never reduce evidence coverage.
211	- Do NOT restructure `SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE`, touch citation rules, or ask
212	  for headings. Additive suffix only.
213	
214	## (c) Outline-agent prompt injection
215	
216	`_call_outline` already implements the full governance mechanism (pack §5;
217	verified): reads `_spec_read(deliverable_spec, "required_sections", [])`, swaps to
218	`OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_REQUIRED` when non-empty, uses required titles as the parse
219	allow-list, and appends `build_requirements_block(deliverable_spec, scope_spec)`
220	(`src/polaris_graph/generator/outline_digest.py` line 909) to the user prompt. The
221	work is **routing discipline**, not new prompt code.
222	
223	**Two-channel routing (the trap both models flag; I adopt it):**
224	- **Hard channel → `required_sections`:** ONLY sections with `title.origin ∈
225	  HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS`, `required=True`, `exact_title_lock=True`. This is exactly
226	  what `from_contract` already puts in `proj.required_titles` (lines 225-256), so
227	  the adapter is `{"required_sections": proj.required_titles}`. Feeding
228	  inferred/kind-derived titles here is a **force leak** — `OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_REQUIRED`
229	  treats the list as authoritative/verbatim/"exactly those, no more, no fewer,"
230	  making an inferred term act HARD.
231	- **Soft channel → guidance line in `build_requirements_block`:** kind-derived /
232	  prefer structure ("Deliverable kind: decision memo — plan bottom-line-first,
233	  options, tradeoffs, recommendation") leaves the domain-selected system prompt and
234	  the default allow-list intact. This is where the kind→role *preferences* live.
235	
236	**Precedence:** (1) explicit ordered contract sections; (2) other hard coverage
237	obligations; (3) kind-profile preferred roles; (4) generic outline behavior.
238	
239	**Coverage-vs-exact-set conflict (kimi's subtlest point; adopt):** `FORCE_PREFER`
240	may never starve coverage, but "EXACTLY those sections, no more" can. Use exact-set
241	semantics only when the union of the required sections'
242	`coverage_requirement_ids` covers all required `CoverageRequirement`s; otherwise
243	allow the outline to append coverage-derived sections after the required ones
244	(disclosed). `SectionRequirement.coverage_requirement_ids` is the resolution key
245	already on the schema. The REQUIRED prompt's undersupply rule ("STILL emit it…
246	disclosed downstream") handles the inverse. **Over-engineering watch:** if the
247	current corpus never exercises this conflict, ship the exact-set path and leave a
248	disclosed TODO — do not build the union-coverage arbiter speculatively.
249	
250	**Gap search** consumes the same stable section/coverage IDs: a gap is "required
251	obligation X is undersupplied," never "the doc is a review, search journals." No
252	kind literal in query control flow.
253	
254	## (d) report_skeleton demoted to safety-net
255	
256	`report_skeleton.py` stays PURE (no I/O, no provenance import) and the post-hoc
257	application in `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py` (~line 17605, `_resolve_archetype`
258	/ `order_report_blocks` / `build_framing_md`, verified present) remains — but
259	**demoted to a fallback / block-order safety layer**:
260	- Outline (§c) determines which substantive sections are composed.
261	- Section advisory (§b) determines how they read.
262	- `order_report_blocks` only PERMUTES already-produced opaque blocks (the multiset
263	  permutation invariant + fail-loud/never-abort fallback to legacy concat is
264	  intact).
265	- `DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE="review"` is used only when no kind resolves (silent contract,
266	  opaque/unmapped kind), disclosed in `assumptions_ledger`. An unmapped raw kind is
267	  preserved verbatim in the projection AND in `resolve_archetype`'s opaque return;
268	  the fallback must not erase it.
269	
270	**Duplicate-structural-section guard (codex; kimi):** a contract-shaped outline may
271	already produce a "Key Findings"/"Bottom Line" or a framing section, while
272	`build_framing_md` / the pipeline Key-Findings block also inject one. Two mitigations:
273	1. **Framing heading** — low risk: template rule 7 forbids the *writer* from
274	   emitting headings, and `build_framing_md` is a claim-free `## {framing_title}`
275	   paragraph. But an *outline required title* equal to the framing title
276	   ("Introduction and Scope") CAN collide. Before adding renderer-owned framing,
277	   check the contract/planned headings and skip the injection if an equivalent
278	   block exists (reuse the Methods-carve-out pattern at line 17636,
279	   `_contract_requires_section`). Contract sections take precedence; skeleton fills
280	   only missing renderer structure.
281	2. **Key Findings / Bottom Line** — reserve those titles for the pipeline KF block;
282	   reject/downgrade a required outline section that duplicates them in section
283	   routing, rather than adding dedup machinery to the skeleton.
284	
285	Do not claim "no conflict" without this guard — that claim would be false (codex).
286	
287	## (e) Faithfulness-safety + OFF byte-identical
288	
289	**`provenance_generator.py` 0-diff.** No signature, call-site, or import change to
290	`strict_verify` / `verify_sentence_provenance` / `require_number_match=True` / span
291	grounding / D8 / comparative-atom recovery. `strict_verify` is a pure function of
292	(sentences, evidence); this design changes neither input. The evidence pool is
293	untouched; the injected advisory contains no checkable atoms (enforced by the
294	no-`\d`/no-`ev_` test in §f). If the model parrots advisory prose, the sentence
295	carries no `[ev_XXX]` marker and the **unchanged** gate drops it — the gate is the
296	backstop, not a casualty. All steering is **upstream** (system-prompt suffix +
297	outline title governance), never a new drop/keep rule.
298	
299	The only two corruption vectors, both closed by construction: (a) injecting factual
300	content into the advisory — forbidden, test-enforced; (b) placing guidance in the
301	*evidence region* of the user prompt where it reads as instruction — avoided by
302	using the **system-prompt** seam (line 4071) exclusively.
303	
304	**Honest scope of "byte-identical":** the *verifier implementation and its
305	treatment of any given (draft, evidence) pair* is unchanged. Prompt steering can
306	change the *draft the model proposes*, so final prose is not guaranteed identical
307	to a prior blind-composition run — say this plainly (codex §5; kimi §5). What IS
308	byte-identical is the **OFF path**.
309	
310	**OFF byte-identical argument:**
311	- `compose_projection=None` ⇒ generator guard at line 9831 skips ⇒
312	  `_voice_advisory_text=""` ⇒ line 4071 `if voice_advisory_text:` false ⇒ system
313	  prompt unchanged.
314	- The projection compiler must yield an **inert** projection (empty strings / empty
315	  lists), and `voice_advisory()` already returns `""` when no voice
316	  (line 161-162) and the doc-type/role additions must return `""` when
317	  `doc_type`/role absent. `from_contract(None)` returns an empty projection
318	  (line 208). Equivalent to passing `None`.
319	- `deliverable_spec=None` ⇒ `_required_sections=[]` ⇒ domain system prompt + default
320	  allow-list + empty requirements block (`build_requirements_block` returns "").
321	- `PG_REPORT_SHAPE=0` ⇒ legacy machinery-first concat (sweep else-branch).
322	- Generic-IR fields serialize only when non-default ⇒ legacy contract SHAs stable.
323	- **Do not** append empty labels ("COMPOSITION CONTRACT:") — even whitespace breaks
324	  the champion prompt hash (codex §6; kimi). Golden-string test asserts byte
325	  equality to HEAD with all three specs None.
326	- Precise claim (kimi): the degraded-path fix INTENTIONALLY changes
327	  *degraded-contract* SHAs (one-time cache miss on `contract_sha256`-keyed
328	  scoping). Disclose it; do not smuggle it as "no change."
329	
330	## (f) Metamorphic cross-deliverable-kind tests + anti-hardcode grep
331	
332	Same question + same evidence; vary ONLY `deliverable.kind`. Assert projection
333	contents AND captured outline/section prompt strings.

335	| Kind | Expected adaptation (archetype key) |
336	|---|---|

338	| `systematic review` / `meta-analysis` | `systematic_review` — review-question/method/synthesis/limitations roles; NO invented PRISMA claim |
339	| `memo` / `decision memo` | `memo` — Bottom-Line/BLUF-first directive, options, tradeoffs, decision-oriented voice |
340	| `policy brief` / `brief` | `brief` — executive-summary framing, concise implications |
341	| `comparison` / `market scan` | `comparison` — Scope & Criteria, symmetric comparison dimensions |
342	| `primer` / `overview` / `explainer` | `explainer` — Overview, concepts/mechanisms, explanatory voice |
343	| `slide deck` (unmapped) | opaque_kind preserved verbatim + DEFAULT review + assumptions-ledger disclosure |
344	
345	Additional assertions:
346	1. **Negative-literal (kills task-72 bleed-through):** memo preamble must NOT
347	   contain "thematic"/"Introduction and Scope"; review preamble must NOT contain
348	   "Bottom Line". Each archetype's directive contains only its own framing.
349	2. **Routing:** explicit locked sections ⇒ `OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_REQUIRED` + exact

354	   `origin ∈ HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS`.
355	4. **OFF golden:** all specs None ⇒ section system prompts + outline user prompts
356	   byte-equal to HEAD capture.

358	6. **Coverage-not-a-heading:** a required *topic* (CoverageRequirement) never
359	   becomes a `required_titles` entry (already enforced by `from_contract`; regress-test it).
360	7. **Skeleton:** permutation multiset invariant holds per kind; duplicate-KF guard
361	   fires when a required section is titled "Bottom Line"/"Key Findings".
362	8. **`strict_verify` call unchanged:** assert it still receives
363	   `require_number_match=True` and the same evidence pool (spy/argument capture).
364	9. **Empty contract == all-None:** `from_contract(empty)` prompts byte-equal to the
365	   None-spec prompts.
366	
367	**Anti-hardcode grep (CI gate):**
368	```bash
369	# No per-kind literal in compose/outline control flow. Kinds live as DATA in
370	# report_skeleton.ARCHETYPES / KIND_SYNONYMS and the compose_directive table.
371	grep -nE '"(memo|brief|review|comparison|explainer|systematic_review|policy brief|market scan|decision memo|literature review)"' \
372	  src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py \
373	  src/polaris_graph/generator/outline_digest.py \

375	  | grep -vE 'ARCHETYPES|KIND_SYNONYMS|import|# data|compose_directive'
376	# Expect: zero hits. Also assert no kind detection off the raw prompt:
377	grep -nE 'prompt.*\.lower\(\).*(review|memo|brief)|in prompt\b' \
378	  src/polaris_graph/planning/research_planning_gate.py
379	# Kind must come from _first_deliverable_kind_value / _DOCTYPE_DIMS off the
380	# contract, never regex on the prompt (kimi #3: "review whether to renew" is a memo).
381	```
382	
383	---
384	
385	## Disagreements + adjudication
386	
387	1. **New projection object vs. reuse (codex `ComposeProjection`; kimi
388	   `to_compose_projection()`) — ADJUDICATE: reuse `ComposeRenderProjection.from_contract`.**
389	   Neither model saw that the object + compiler already exist and already read
390	   doc_type/voice/sections. Building a second one is redundant and risks a second
391	   synonym/dim table that drifts. Extend the existing `voice_advisory()`/add a
392	   sibling; add thin `deliverable_spec`/`scope_spec` adapters. This is the single
393	   biggest correction and it shrinks the diff.
394	
395	2. **Kind-derived default sections — codex (never manufacture `sections`) vs. kimi
396	   (allow inferred, unlocked, disclosed skeleton when kind is explicit) —
397	   ADJUDICATE: codex's stricter line.** Keep `contract.sections` for explicit user
398	   titles only; kind→role preferences ride the soft outline channel (§c) as
399	   guidance, not as `SectionRequirement`s. Removes an invention surface and is
400	   simpler; `from_contract` already ignores non-locked sections for
401	   `required_titles`, so an inferred skeleton would only muddy the object.
402	
403	3. **Global advisory string vs. per-section role — codex/kimi agree it must be
404	   per-section; the pack's current seam threads one global `_voice_advisory_text`.
405	   ADJUDICATE: two additive layers** — global doc-type framing in
406	   `_voice_advisory_text`, per-section role appended at the `_run_section`
407	   boundary. Both inert when empty.
408	
409	4. **Coverage-vs-exact-set arbiter — kimi builds it; codex doesn't mention it.
410	   ADJUDICATE: specify it, gate on need.** Correct and real, but only if the corpus
411	   exercises the conflict. Ship exact-set + disclosed fallback; build the
412	   union-coverage arbiter only when a test actually hits it (over-engineering watch).
413	
414	## Over-engineering watch
415	
416	- **Do NOT** add a new `ComposeProjection` class / `to_compose_projection()` method
417	  — reuse `from_contract`. (Both models would have.)
418	- **Do NOT** manufacture `contract.sections` from the kind — invention surface.
419	- **Do NOT** build the union-coverage / exact-set arbiter until a test hits it.
420	- **Do NOT** promote `_DOCTYPE_DIMS`/voice dims into a *third* copy in the gate —
421	  import the one in `compose_render_projection.py` (or publicize it), single source.
422	- **Do NOT** hoist `ARCHETYPES`/`KIND_SYNONYMS` into a new shared module unless the
423	  import from `report_skeleton.py` (pure) proves circular — a plain import is
424	  cheaper than a new module.
425	- **Do NOT** add dedup machinery to `report_skeleton` — reserve KF/Bottom-Line
426	  titles upstream in routing instead.
427	- Keep `length_note` advisory; do not turn it into a truncation gate.
428	- Resist expanding the section advisory into paragraphs — a few directive lines;
429	  every extra sentence is prompt-hash surface and verbosity risk.
430	
431	## Faithfulness/OFF verdict
432	
433	`faithfulness_safe = true` — all steering upstream; no claims/citations injected;
434	`provenance_generator.py` 0-diff; gate is the unchanged backstop.
435	`off_byte_identical = true` — for the OFF path (all specs None / `PG_REPORT_SHAPE=0`).
436	Degraded-contract SHAs intentionally change (disclosed), and steered drafts are not
437	guaranteed prose-identical to prior blind runs — the byte-identical claim is scoped
438	to the OFF path and to the verifier's treatment of a fixed (draft, evidence) pair.

229	    plain_required: list[str] = []
230	    any_order = False
231	    sections = getattr(contract, "sections", None)
232	    if isinstance(sections, (list, tuple)):
233	        for idx, sec in enumerate(sections):
234	            if not getattr(sec, "exact_title_lock", False):
235	                continue
236	            title = _term_value_text(getattr(sec, "title", None))
237	            if not title:
238	                continue
239	            order = getattr(sec, "order", None)
240	            if isinstance(order, int):
241	                titles_with_order.append((order, title))
242	                any_order = True
243	            else:
244	                titles_with_order.append((idx + 10_000, title))  # keep source order
245	            plain_required.append(title)
246	    # de-dup preserving the (order, then source) sort.
247	    seen: set[str] = set()
248	    ordered_titles: list[str] = []
249	    for _, title in sorted(titles_with_order, key=lambda x: x[0]):
250	        low = title.lower()
251	        if low in seen:
252	            continue
253	        seen.add(low)
254	        ordered_titles.append(title)
255	    proj.required_titles = ordered_titles
256	    proj.ordered = any_order and bool(ordered_titles)
257	
258	    # length as PLANNING CONTEXT — a reader-facing note only, never a cap.
259	    proj.length_note = _length_note(deliverable)
260	
261	    return proj
262	
263	
264	def from_artifact(artifact: Any) -> ComposeRenderProjection:
265	    """Compile from a pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact`."""
266	    if artifact is None:
267	        return ComposeRenderProjection()
268	    return from_contract(getattr(artifact, "contract", None))
269	
270	
271	def _length_note(deliverable: Any) -> str:
272	    """A reader-facing planning note derived from any length term. NEVER a cap.
273	
274	    Returns e.g. ``"target ~2000 words"`` — surfaced as context for the writer /
275	    render metadata only. An absent length yields ``""``.
276	    """
277	    if not isinstance(deliverable, (list, tuple)):
278	        return ""
279	    for t in deliverable:
280	        dim = _norm(getattr(t, "dimension", "")).lower()
281	        if "length" not in dim:
282	            continue
283	        txt = _term_value_text(t)
284	        if txt:
285	            return f"planning context (not a hard cap): {dim.rsplit('.', 1)[-1]}={txt}"
286	    return ""
287	
288	
289	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
290	# Convenience: the compose-side entrypoint used by the generator seam.
291	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
292	
293	
294	def compose_voice_advisory(compose_projection: Any) -> str:
295	    """The prose-only voice advisory for a compose projection (or ``""``).
296	
297	    Accepts either a :class:`ComposeRenderProjection` or a duck-typed object with
298	    a ``voice_advisory()`` method, or ``None``. Fail-open: anything unusable =>
299	    ``""`` => the generator's advisory append is inert => byte-identical.
300	    """
301	    if compose_projection is None:
302	        return ""
303	    fn = getattr(compose_projection, "voice_advisory", None)
304	    if callable(fn):
305	        try:
306	            return _norm(fn())
307	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open
308	            return ""
309	    return ""

454	    prompt: str, candidates: list[CandidateConstraint]
455	) -> ResearchContract:
456	    """The LOSSLESS fallback (PG_GATE ON): the deterministic-authoritative core
457	    run as the WHOLE contract when the LLM failed.
458	
459	    Every span-verified deterministic explicit source/date/exclusion constraint is
460	    authored (via the registry, canonical dimension, correct force), and every
461	    content-coverage candidate becomes a coverage requirement. Deliverable/format/
462	    length/tone candidates land in the DELIVERABLE group — NEVER scope (no category
463	    leakage). An unmappable kind is preserved as a first-class OPAQUE term. Marked
464	    ``compiler_degraded=true``: enrichment is thin, but NO explicit constraint
465	    vanished.
466	    """
467	    objective = [ContractTerm(
468	        term_id="objective.question",
469	        dimension="objective.question",
470	        value=prompt.strip(),
471	        origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if prompt.strip() else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
472	        force=FORCE_OPEN,
473	        spans=[PromptSpan(0, len(prompt), prompt)] if prompt.strip() else [],
474	        rationale="lossless fallback: raw request retained as the objective",
475	    )]
476	
477	    # authoritative source/date/exclusion terms + content-coverage terms.
478	    scope, content = _author_deterministic_terms(candidates, prompt)
479	    coverage: list[CoverageRequirement] = [
480	        CoverageRequirement(
481	            requirement_id=t.term_id, kind="topic", statement=t, required=True,
482	        )
483	        for t in content
484	    ]
485	
486	    # deliverable / rhetoric candidates: file by stage owner, NEVER in scope.
487	    deliverable: list[ContractTerm] = []
488	    n = 0
489	    for cand in candidates:
490	        if cand.dimension not in (
491	            "deliverable.format", "deliverable.length", "rhetoric.tone",
492	        ):
493	            continue
494	        span_ok = _cand_span_ok(cand, prompt)
495	        if not (cand.value or "").strip():
496	            continue
497	        n += 1
498	        deliverable.append(ContractTerm(
499	            term_id=f"deliverable.cand_{n}",
500	            dimension=cand.canonical_dimension(),
501	            value=cand.value,
502	            origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if span_ok else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
503	            force=FORCE_PREFER,   # deliverable shape is a preference, never a gate
504	            spans=_cand_spans(cand, prompt),
505	            subject=cand.subject,
506	            attribute=cand.attribute,
507	            stage_owner=cand.stage_owner,
508	            normalization_status=cand.normalization_status or NORM_EXACT,
509	            rationale="lossless fallback: deliverable shape preserved (render-owned)",
510	        ))
511	
512	    return ResearchContract(
513	        objective=objective,
514	        scope=scope,
515	        deliverable=deliverable,
516	        coverage=coverage,
517	        complexity="degraded",
518	        compiler_degraded=True,
519	    )
520	
521	
522	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
523	# DETERMINISTIC-AUTHORITATIVE CORE + MONOTONIC MERGE (Phase B — the inversion)
524	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
525	#
526	# This REPLACES the deleted ``_promote_source_scope`` task-shaped whitelist. The
527	# authority is INVERTED: deterministic code (the S0 candidate adapter + the
528	# candidate→canonical registry) AUTHORS the explicit-constraint contract; the LLM
529	# is additive-only and MERGED monotonically (it may add/enrich, never delete,
530	# downgrade, or re-dimension a deterministic explicit term).
531	#
532	# No per-type branching: every source/date/exclusion/coverage family is driven by
533	# ``candidate_adapter.CANDIDATE_REGISTRY`` (the row supplies canonical dimension +
534	# subject/attribute/operator/stage_owner). An unknown kind stays a first-class
535	# OPAQUE term — preserved, never dropped.
536	#
537	# Gated behind PG_GATE (default OFF): with the flag OFF this whole core is a no-op
538	# and the compiled contract is byte-identical to the pre-inversion path (the LLM
539	# compile alone), preserving the OFF guardrail.
540	
541	# The candidate dimensions whose terms are EXPLICIT authority (a real user rule
542	# with a verbatim span) — as opposed to advisory coverage the LLM may reshape.
543	# A span-verified candidate on any of these is authored as an explicit contract
544	# term the LLM merge can neither drop nor downgrade.
545	_AUTHORITATIVE_DIMENSIONS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
546	    "source.types", "source.quality", "source.language", "source.scope_facet",
547	    "source.jurisdiction", "source.named", "source.named_exclude", "date.recency",
548	    "content.exclusion",
549	})
550	
551	
552	def _cand_span_ok(cand: CandidateConstraint, prompt: str) -> bool:
553	    """A candidate carries at least one verbatim-quote-verified span."""
554	    return bool(cand.spans) and any(
555	        0 <= sp.start <= sp.end <= len(prompt) and sp.quote == prompt[sp.start:sp.end]
556	        for sp in cand.spans
557	    )

class ResearchContract:
    """The typed contract. Grouped as objective / scope / content / deliverable.

    To keep the schema compact and fail-soft, the grouped specs are held as flat
    ``ContractTerm`` lists keyed by ``dimension`` (e.g. ``objective.question``,
    ``scope.source_types``, ``scope.date``, ``deliverable.kind``) plus the two
    structured collections that need their own shape (``coverage`` and
    ``sections``). Every term is uniformly a :class:`ContractTerm`, so the
    validator's no-invention sweep is a single pass over :meth:`all_terms`.
    """

    schema_version: str = SCHEMA_VERSION
    objective: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
    scope: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
    content_terms: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
    deliverable: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
    coverage: list[CoverageRequirement] = field(default_factory=list)
    sections: list[SectionRequirement] = field(default_factory=list)
    ambiguities: list[Ambiguity] = field(default_factory=list)
    assumptions: list[Assumption] = field(default_factory=list)
    conflicts: list[ContractConflict] = field(default_factory=list)
    complexity: str = "standard"
    compiler_degraded: bool = False

    def all_terms(self) -> list[ContractTerm]:
        """Every :class:`ContractTerm` in the contract (for the validator sweep)."""
        terms: list[ContractTerm] = []
        terms.extend(self.objective)
        terms.extend(self.scope)
        terms.extend(self.content_terms)
        terms.extend(self.deliverable)
        for cr in self.coverage:
            terms.append(cr.statement)
            terms.extend(cr.compare_on)
        for sec in self.sections:
            terms.append(sec.title)
        return terms

    def hard_terms(self) -> list[ContractTerm]:
        return [t for t in self.all_terms() if t.is_hard()]

    # -- S2 retrieval-projection compile methods ------------------------------
    # Thin wrappers over ``retrieval_projection`` (the no-starvation core). Kept
    # on the contract per the S2 task ("contract.to_research_frame() /
    # to_scope_terms() compile methods"); they build a projection over THIS
    # contract with an empty plan (scope routing needs only the contract) so a
    # caller holding just a contract can still route backends. The full
    # query-text projection (which needs the plan's mandatory intents) is built
    # via ``retrieval_projection.from_artifact`` / ``from_contract_and_plan``.
    # Deferred import keeps this module free of the ResearchFrame dependency.

    def to_research_frame(self, plan: Any = None) -> Any:
        """Route this contract's scope onto a champion ``ResearchFrame`` (or
        ``None`` when nothing is routable — the byte-identical champion path)."""
        from src.polaris_graph.planning.retrieval_projection import (  # noqa: PLC0415
            from_contract_and_plan,
        )
        proj = from_contract_and_plan(self, plan or ResearchExecutionPlan())
        return proj.to_research_frame()

    def to_scope_terms(self, plan: Any = None) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
        """The hard/soft scope split + language lanes + routed evidence needs."""
        from src.polaris_graph.planning.retrieval_projection import (  # noqa: PLC0415
            from_contract_and_plan,
        )
        proj = from_contract_and_plan(self, plan or ResearchExecutionPlan())
        return proj.to_scope_terms()

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "schema_version": self.schema_version,
            "objective": [t.to_dict() for t in self.objective],
            "scope": [t.to_dict() for t in self.scope],

def _norm(s: Any) -> str:
    return str(s or "").strip().lower()


def resolve_archetype_key(kind_value: Any) -> "tuple[str, bool, str]":
    """Resolve a raw ``deliverable.kind`` VALUE string onto an archetype KEY.

    Returns ``(key, assumed, opaque_kind)`` mirroring :func:`resolve_archetype`
    but reading a bare kind string instead of a contract (so a projection that
    already carries ``doc_type`` need not reconstruct a contract). ``assumed`` is
    ``True`` when the default key was used (empty or unmapped kind); ``opaque_kind``
    is the verbatim unmapped kind (``""`` otherwise). PURE. Same longest-synonym-
    wins matching as :func:`resolve_archetype` — no new vocabulary, no per-kind
    branch."""
    raw = str(kind_value or "").strip()
    v = _norm(raw)
    if not v:
        return DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE, True, ""
    best_key = ""
    best_len = -1
    for syn, key in KIND_SYNONYMS.items():
        synl = syn.strip().lower()
        if not synl:
            continue
        if synl in v or v in synl:
            if len(synl) > best_len:
                best_key = key
                best_len = len(synl)
    if best_key and best_key in ARCHETYPES:
        return best_key, False, ""
    return DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE, True, raw


def compose_doc_type_directive(kind_value: Any) -> str:
    """The per-archetype doc-type framing directive for a raw ``deliverable.kind``
    VALUE (``""`` when the kind is empty).

    Resolves ``kind_value`` -> archetype key (via :func:`resolve_archetype_key`)
    and returns the matching :data:`COMPOSE_DIRECTIVES` string. An EMPTY kind
    yields ``""`` (inert => byte-identical). An UNMAPPED kind falls back to the
    default archetype's directive (the caller discloses the assumption). DIRECTIVE
    ONLY: no fact, no digit, no ``ev_`` id, no heading. PURE."""
    if not str(kind_value or "").strip():
        return ""
    key, _assumed, _opaque = resolve_archetype_key(kind_value)
    return COMPOSE_DIRECTIVES.get(key, COMPOSE_DIRECTIVES[DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE])

# EXPLICIT, user-named section titles -> SectionRequirement objects (lossless).
    # Only candidates whose CANONICAL dimension names a section become sections; a
    # required *topic* (content.coverage) is NEVER a heading. exact_title_lock is
    # honored ONLY when the title term's origin is in HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS — we route
    # through ``SectionRequirement.from_dict`` so its downgrade rule (no inferred lock)
    # is the single source of truth and the constructor cannot bypass it. Sections are
    # NEVER manufactured from the kind (kind->role are soft outline preferences, not
    # locks). Verbatim title/origin/span/encounter-order preserved.
    sections: list[SectionRequirement] = []
    s = 0
    for cand in candidates:
        if cand.canonical_dimension() != "deliverable.section":
            continue
        title_val = (cand.value or "").strip()
        if not title_val:
            continue
        span_ok = _cand_span_ok(cand, prompt)
        s += 1
        title_origin = ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if span_ok else ORIGIN_INFERRED
        title_term = ContractTerm(
            term_id=f"deliverable.section_{s}",
            dimension="deliverable.section",
            value=title_val,
            origin=title_origin,
            force=FORCE_HARD if span_ok else FORCE_PREFER,
            spans=_cand_spans(cand, prompt),
            subject=cand.subject,
            attribute=cand.attribute,
            stage_owner=cand.stage_owner,
            normalization_status=cand.normalization_status or NORM_EXACT,
            rationale="lossless fallback: explicit user-named section preserved",
        )
        # from_dict re-applies the downgrade rule (exact_title_lock only for a
        # HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS title); a claimed lock on an inferred title is dropped.
        sections.append(SectionRequirement.from_dict({
            "section_id": f"deliverable.section_{s}",
            "title": title_term.to_dict(),
            "order": cand.detail.get("order") if isinstance(cand.detail, dict) else None,
            "required": True,
            "exact_title_lock": span_ok,
        }))

    return ResearchContract(

def _deliverable_spec_from(compose_proj: "Any") -> "dict[str, Any] | None":
    """Thin ADAPTER: the outline seam's ``deliverable_spec`` view of a compiled
    ``ComposeRenderProjection`` (S4 compose injection).

    HARD channel -> ``required_sections`` is EXACTLY ``proj.required_titles`` (only
    exact-title-locked, origin-in-HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS titles; a kind-derived title
    NEVER lands here — that would be a force leak). SOFT channel -> ``kind_guidance``
    carries the deliverable-kind framing as a preference line for
    ``build_requirements_block`` (leaving the domain system prompt + default allow-list
    intact). ``audience``/``tone`` ride the existing advisory lines. Returns ``None``
    when the projection is empty/None => the generator's outline seam is byte-identical.
    """
    if compose_proj is None:
        return None
    try:
        required = [str(t).strip() for t in (getattr(compose_proj, "required_titles", []) or []) if str(t).strip()]
        audience = str(getattr(compose_proj, "audience", "") or "").strip()
        tone = str(getattr(compose_proj, "tone", "") or "").strip()
        length_note = str(getattr(compose_proj, "length_note", "") or "").strip()
        # SOFT kind guidance: the doc-type framing directive (preference only). Empty
        # unless a doc_type resolves. This is guidance, never a required-title lock.
        kind_guidance = ""
        _dfn = getattr(compose_proj, "doc_type_directive", None)
        if callable(_dfn):
            kind_guidance = str(_dfn() or "").strip()
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: a spec fault => None => byte-identical outline
        return None
    if not (required or audience or tone or length_note or kind_guidance):

    spec: "dict[str, Any]" = {}
    if required:
        spec["required_sections"] = required
    if audience:
        spec["audience"] = audience
    if tone:
        spec["tone"] = tone
    if length_note:
        spec["length_target"] = length_note
    if kind_guidance:
        spec["kind_guidance"] = kind_guidance
    return spec or None
